overview: Based on the published agenda, the D.C. Everest Area School District Board of Education was scheduled to hold a regular meeting on June 17, 2026, that included a public hearing regarding a waiver of instructional minutes for Riverside Elementary. The meeting was expected to address multiple action items including staff compensation plans for 2026-2027, the 2026-2027 budget update, student services reports, and approval of out-of-state trips for junior high and senior students.
committee: Regular School Board Meeting Including a Public Hearing

discussions.item: Public Hearing re: Waiver of Minutes for Riverside Elementary
discussions.body: The board was scheduled to hold a public hearing as required under Wisconsin Statute 118.38(1)(b) regarding a waiver of instructional minutes for Riverside Elementary. Supporting materials included a Notice of Public Hearing and waiver application documents.

Presenters Kelly Ziegelbauer (Weston Principal), Lauren Doescher, and Katie Wochinski were scheduled to present information regarding a 'Soaring into Summer' initiative, with supporting presentation materials provided.

Dr. Lindell and Gina Lehman were scheduled to present annual required student data reports covering homeless and foster care status, seclusion and restraint incidents, crime statistics on school property, nursing reports, at-risk students nearing graduation, continuum of services, and emergency support services. The presentation was expected to include multiple supporting documents detailing student services and student support outcomes.
Aaron Hoffman and student Takoda Rowland were scheduled to present a report on the Education for Employment and Alternative Career Pathway programs for the district.

discussions.item: Resolution for Riverside Elementary Waiver of Minutes
discussions.body: Dr. Strike was scheduled to present a resolution regarding the waiver of instructional hours for Riverside Elementary, with supporting documents including the waiver application and resolution language for board consideration.

discussions.item: Junior High and Idea Out-of-State Trips
discussions.body: Dr. Lindell was scheduled to present three out-of-state trip requests: a junior high trip to Washington, D.C. for 2026-27, a junior high world language trip, and an Idea senior class trip, with supporting documentation for each proposal.
Dr. Strike was scheduled to present the 2026-2027 staff compensation plans for board consideration, with supporting materials outlining compensation proposals for certified staff.
Dr. Strike was scheduled to provide a comprehensive update on the 2026-2027 budget, including preliminary budget presentation materials for board review and discussion.
Dr. Strike was scheduled to present a year-end spending report for the 2025-2026 fiscal year, providing financial details on district expenditures as the school year concludes.
Dr. Strike was scheduled to request board approval for 2026-2027 budgetary spending authority, with supporting documentation outlining the proposed spending framework.

Dr. Nye, Dr. Lindell, and Dr. Strike were scheduled to present first readings of seven policies including Definitions (po0100), Public Comment at Board Meetings (po0167.3), Employment of Professional Staff (po3120), Employment of Personnel in Community Education Programs (po3120.05), Non-Renewal, Resignation, and Termination (po3140), Employment of Substitutes (po4120.04), and Full-Time Student (po5200.01), with a memo regarding credit limits for senior high students.

publicComment: A public comment period was included on this agenda at item VI.
actionItems: Board was expected to vote on the Resolution for Riverside Elementary Waiver of Minutes following the public hearing
Board was expected to approve the Staff Compensation Plan for 2026-2027
Board was expected to approve the 2026-2027 Budgetary Spending Approval
Board was expected to approve out-of-state trips for junior high and senior students
Board was expected to consider first readings of seven policies with second readings anticipated at a future meeting
Board was expected to approve Second Reading of Policies po5136 Personal Communication Devices and po9215 School Support Organizations
title: Regular School Board Meeting Including a Public Hearing as Required Underneath Wis. Stat. 118.38(1)(b) - 2026-06-17
